data.short_description: Morán Honduras Restaurant has reopened under new ownership at 534 Middle Turnpike East in Manchester. The restaurant closed briefly to transfer to new owners and has now reopened. The menu features nachos, burritos, quesadillas, pupusas, tacos, Hispanic breakfast, and more.

data.location: 117 Washington Avenue, North Haven (Washington Commons shopping center)
data.short_description: 7 Brew Coffee has set its official opening date of June 8th for its new North Haven location at 117 Washington Avenue in the Washington Commons shopping center. The drive-thru coffee shop will host pre-opening community events including a "Caffeine for a Cause" fundraiser weekend and Friends & Family Weekend with free drinks. 7 Brew serves coffee, energy drinks, smoothies, milkshakes, teas, lemonades, and more.

The Wok Chinese Kitchen is coming soon to the former Five Guys restaurant space at 440 Boston Post Road in Orange. The new fast-casual Chinese restaurant will serve dishes such as Orange Chicken, General Tso's Chicken, Mongolian Beef, and more. It is a growing chain with three locations in Pennsylvania and another coming soon in New Jersey.

Five Guys at 440 Boston Post Road in Orange closed abruptly in late 2025 after over two decades of business. The space is now being taken over by The Wok Chinese Kitchen. No further details on the closure are provided.

Two Trees Inn has officially reopened following a ribbon cutting with the Mashantucket Pequot Tribal Nation and new operator Waterford Hotel Group. The hotel at 240 Indiantown Road in Ledyard features 280 guest rooms, an indoor pool, fitness center, walking trails, on-site breakfast, and a bar. The property is located next door to Foxwoods Resort Casino and Great Wolf Lodge.

data.status: No qualifying business opening or closure
data.short_description: This article is a restaurant review of Wen Ming Taste's new AYCE (all-you-can-eat) menu option at an already-operating location. The Chinese & Japanese restaurant at 89 West Road in Ellington now offers AYCE sushi, appetizers, and Chinese entrees. No opening or closing event is described.

data.location: 1735 Ellington Road, South Windsor (Town Center Shopping Center)
data.status: opening soon / anticipated to open on May 23rd
data.short_description: Turning Times Pottery is a new pottery studio opening soon at 1735 Ellington Road in South Windsor's Town Center Shopping Center. The studio will offer walk-in painting, reservations, private parties, events, themed workshops, and take-home pottery kits. It is owned by Janelle Zahner, a self-taught potter who left a 19-year corporate career to pursue ceramics full-time.

Article covers a proposed mixed-use development at 1170 Main Street in Newington (former Keeney Manufacturing site), including 244 apartments, retail, a performing arts theater, and amenity space. The proposal has not been approved; only a pre-application discussion is scheduled. No specific business opening or closure is reported.

data.store_name: Furnish Cheap by Universal Hotel Furniture Liquidators
data.location: 470 Lewis Avenue, Meriden (Meriden Mall, former Dick's Sporting Goods location)
data.short_description: Furnish Cheap by Universal Hotel Furniture Liquidators has opened at the Meriden Mall in the former Dick's Sporting Goods space at 470 Lewis Avenue. The store sells commercial-grade hotel furnishings including decor, appliances, beds, and couches at wholesale prices to the public. The business has been in operation for decades, previously located in West Haven.

Paris Baguette, a French-inspired Korean bakery chain, is coming soon to the first floor of Danbury Fair mall in the spaces previously occupied by Chipotle and Fiza Pakistani & Indian Cuisine. The bakery is currently under construction and will offer pastries, cakes, sandwiches, espresso beverages, and more. This will be the chain's second Connecticut location after opening in West Hartford in late 2025.

data.location: 34 Shunpike Road, Cromwell (Cross Roads Plaza, former Kuyi Sushi space)
data.short_description: Wingstop is coming soon to 34 Shunpike Road in Cromwell in the former Kuyi Sushi space within Cross Roads Plaza. The chicken wing-centric restaurant will occupy 3,060 square feet and no opening timeline has been announced. Wingstop is expanding across Connecticut with additional locations coming soon to East Hartford and Branford.

data.location: 9 Dog Lane, Suites 109 and 110, Storrs (Storrs Center)
data.short_description: Monmouth Cards is coming soon to Suites 109 and 110 at 9 Dog Lane in Storrs Center, next door to Jefferson Fry Company. The store will buy, sell, and trade sports cards, Pokémon, TCG, and other trading cards, and will also host in-store events. No opening timeline has been announced; this will be the chain's third Connecticut location.

Soft Serve Factory is now open at 700 Watertown Avenue in Waterbury in the former Carvel space next to Frankie's, having opened on May 10, 2026. The locally owned shop has served the community for over 15 years, previously operating under the Carvel brand. They serve 18 flavors of hard serve ice cream, soft serve, non-dairy options, and frozen dessert formats including cones, sundaes, and milkshakes.

The Carvel location at 700 Watertown Avenue in Waterbury has closed and been replaced by Soft Serve Factory. No specific closure date is mentioned in the article. The new operator previously ran the site under the Carvel brand for over 15 years.

Article reports that the Hell's Kitchen TV show will return to film seasons 25 & 26 at Foxwoods Resort Casino on a sound stage. No business opening or closure is described; the article covers a TV production announcement.
